headline: Wednesday 15-game slate — one confirmed short-outing lean: Christian Scott
body: Scanning all 30 Wednesday starters for a CONFIRMED short-outing reason, one clean edge stands out. Christian Scott (NYM @ MIL) is building back from hybrid Tommy John surgery that cost him all of 2025 — the Mets are managing his workload and he has not worked deep in nine starts (2.87 ERA over 40.2 IP, ~4.5 IP per start with several four-inning outings), so ~4 IP is the expectation. Note: Max Scherzer appears on some probable-pitcher grids for Toronto, but he is actually making a Triple-A rehab start (60-65 pitches) on 7/22 and is NOT the Blue Jays' MLB starter today, so he is excluded. Everyone else defers to the market: Roki Sasaki, Cade Cavalli and Griffin Jax are all past their build-ups or fully stretched; rookies Gabriel Hughes, Logan Henderson and Gage Jump have no announced per-start caps; and the front-line arms — Sale, Cole, Nola, Alcantara, Williams — work deep with no cap. Tendencies, not guarantees; verify starters at first pitch; flat small units, never chase.

headline: Tuesday 15-game slate — three confirmed short-outing leans: Drake, Schultz, Rojas
body: Scanning all 30 Tuesday starters for a CONFIRMED short-outing reason, three clean edges stand out. Kohl Drake (ARI vs ATH) makes his MLB debut on a stated ~70-pitch limit (could stretch to ~80) — not built up to go deep, so ~4 IP is the expectation. Noah Schultz (CWS @ TEX) makes his first start back from a knee (patellar tendinitis) IL stint; his rehab only built him to a ~65-75 pitch ceiling, so a short leash is expected (~4 IP). Kendry Rojas (MIN @ CLE) is a rookie the Twins are openly managing — he has not exceeded 4 IP / 60 pitches in any 2026 outing, so ~4 IP again. Everyone else defers to the market: Luinder Avila and Tyler Phillips are reliever-turned-starters but have reached ~5.2 IP with no announced cap; Kumar Rocker and Walker Buehler are past their build-ups; and the front-line arms — Wheeler, Burns, Gausman, Castillo, Valdez — work deep with no cap. Tendencies, not guarantees; verify starters at first pitch; flat small units, never chase.

headline: Sunday slate — one clean edge: Rangel's first career start; everyone else defers to market
body: Scanning all ~31 Sunday starters (incl. the LAD@NYY split doubleheader from Saturday's rainout) for a CONFIRMED short-outing reason, one clean edge stands out: Alan Rangel (PHI vs NYM) makes his FIRST career MLB start — a career swingman/bulk reliever whose recent outings top out around 5 IP. The Phillies confirmed they won't use an opener, so Rangel starts conventionally, but he isn't stretched to go deep; ~4 IP is the expectation. Nobody else qualifies: Hunter Greene is back from elbow surgery but Francona said flatly he WON'T limit him; Eury Perez and Shohei Ohtani are both past their TJ build-ups and fully stretched (~6+ IP); and the Yankees' DH Game 2 starter is still officially TBD (watch for a possible bullpen game, but unconfirmed). Everyone else — Skenes, Gray, Yamamoto, McClanahan, Eovaldi, Gilbert, Brown and the rest — defers to the market. Tendencies, not guarantees; verify starters at first pitch; flat small units, never chase.

headline: Full 16-game Saturday slate — Sandoval the lone real edge, three situational spot-starts
body: Second-half Saturday brings a 16-game board (a PIT@CLE doubleheader after Friday's air-quality postponement). Scanning all 30 starters for a CONFIRMED short-outing reason, one clean edge stands out: Patrick Sandoval (BOS vs TB) is making just his 2nd start back from a 2-year Tommy John recovery — his debut was 4.1 IP on 65 pitches with a stated ~68-80 pitch ceiling, so ~4 IP is the expectation. Three situational spot-starts also lean short: Randy Dobnak (KC vs SD) makes his first start of the year after two relief outings; Logan Allen (CLE Game 2) was called up from Triple-A that day to start the nightcap; and Owen Murphy (ATL vs TEX) makes his first career MLB start after relief-only work — though the book already prices much of the rookie/spot-start piece. Everyone else defers to the market: Ian Seymour, Jared Jones, Emmet Sheehan, Shane Bieber and Rhett Lowder are all past their build-ups with no announced caps. Tendencies, not guarantees; verify starters at first pitch; flat small units, never chase.
